thema: CEFR B1 · Article: Green commuting to school
blockJson:
{"id":"b1","punkte":20,"arbeitsanweisung":"Write an article for the school magazine. Explain how students could travel to school more sustainably and make your recommendations convincing.","typ":"offeneSchreibaufgabe","config":{"situation":"The following source is fictional: A survey by the fictional Riverside Secondary School found that 68% of students come to school by car, while only 12% cycle. The school magazine Green Steps is preparing an issue about sustainable everyday choices. Write an article for students aged 14 to 16.","textsorte":"article","umfangWorte":{"min":200,"max":250},"aspekte":["explain two reasons why the current travel pattern matters","present at least two realistic alternatives and compare their advantages","end with a clear recommendation for the school community"]},"loesung":{"musterloesung":"Rethinking the way we travel to school

The fictional Riverside survey gives us a reason to rethink our morning routine. If 68% of students arrive by car, the school area becomes crowded and families produce unnecessary emissions. The survey also shows that cycling is still uncommon, even though many students live close enough to ride safely.

One solution would be a weekly walking and cycling day. Students could travel together in small groups, which would make the journey safer and more enjoyable. Public transport is another useful option for students who live farther away. It may take a little longer, but it reduces the number of cars around the school.

Riverside should start with a trial month and provide secure bicycle parking. The school could also publish clear bus information and recognise classes that take part. Sustainable travel should not be presented as a punishment: it should become a practical and positive choice.","erwartungshorizont":{"inhalt":"The article explains why the fictional survey matters, compares at least two realistic travel alternatives and ends with a clear recommendation.","struktur":"The text has a recognisable headline, an opening based on the survey, logically ordered paragraphs and a purposeful conclusion.","ausdruck":"The article uses an accessible B1 register, varied linking words and concrete vocabulary for transport, emissions and school measures.","sprachrichtigkeit":"Sentence structures, verb forms, spelling and punctuation are sufficiently accurate for a clear B1 article."}}}
quelleHinweis: Redaktionell kuratierte Aufgabe für das Fachpaket Englisch Oberstufe (CEFR B1–B2), fiktives Unterrichtsszenario. Kein amtliches Lehrplanmaterial; Lehrplankonformität nicht geprüft.

thema: CEFR B1 · Formal email: Exchange week information
blockJson:
{"id":"b1","punkte":20,"arbeitsanweisung":"Write a formal email to the project coordinator. Ask for the missing information, explain one personal preference and suggest one activity for the exchange week.","typ":"offeneSchreibaufgabe","config":{"situation":"The following message is fictional: Dear students, our fictional partner school in Brighton will host an exchange week in May. The programme will include lessons, a city walk and one afternoon activity, but the accommodation, daily timetable and activity choices have not yet been announced. Write a formal email to Ms Taylor, the fictional project coordinator.","textsorte":"formal email","umfangWorte":{"min":200,"max":250},"aspekte":["use an appropriate subject line, greeting and closing","ask clearly about accommodation and the timetable","state one preference and make one polite suggestion"]},"loesung":{"musterloesung":"Subject: Questions about the Brighton exchange week

Dear Ms Taylor,

I am writing to ask for some further information about the exchange week in May. Could you please tell me whether students will stay with host families or in a school residence? I would also be grateful if you could send us a provisional daily timetable, because my parents need to plan the journey and the return time.

I would prefer an activity connected with local culture, such as a guided visit to a museum. It would also be helpful if the programme included some time for students from both schools to work together on a small project.

Thank you for your help. I look forward to hearing from you.

Kind regards,
Alex Morgan","erwartungshorizont":{"inhalt":"The email asks about accommodation and the timetable, states one personal preference and gives one relevant suggestion.","struktur":"The email has a suitable subject line, formal greeting, clear paragraphs, a polite closing and an appropriate sign-off.","ausdruck":"The writer uses polite request forms, an appropriate formal register and clear linking expressions.","sprachrichtigkeit":"Grammar, spelling, punctuation and sentence structure are sufficiently accurate for a clear B1 formal email."}}}
quelleHinweis: Redaktionell kuratierte Aufgabe für das Fachpaket Englisch Oberstufe (CEFR B1–B2), fiktives Unterrichtsszenario. Kein amtliches Lehrplanmaterial; Lehrplankonformität nicht geprüft.

thema: CEFR B1 · Linking words and their communicative functions
blockJson: {"id":"b1","punkte":10,"arbeitsanweisung":"Match each linking word or phrase with its communicative function. Two options remain unused.","typ":"matching","config":{"items":[{"nr":1,"prompt":"however"},{"nr":2,"prompt":"because"},{"nr":3,"prompt":"in addition"},{"nr":4,"prompt":"for example"},{"nr":5,"prompt":"as a result"}],"optionen":[{"key":"A","text":"gives a reason"},{"key":"B","text":"adds another point"},{"key":"C","text":"introduces a result"},{"key":"D","text":"shows contrast"},{"key":"E","text":"introduces an example"},{"key":"F","text":"summarises the whole text"},{"key":"G","text":"introduces a condition"}]},"loesung":{"zuordnung":{"1":"D","2":"A","3":"B","4":"E","5":"C"}}}

thema: CEFR B1 · Formal and informal register
blockJson: {"id":"b1","punkte":12,"arbeitsanweisung":"Classify each expression as formal or informal register. Then rewrite one informal expression in a formal way.","typ":"kategorisierung","config":{"items":[{"nr":1,"text":"I am writing to enquire about the course.","optionen":["formal","informal"]},{"nr":2,"text":"Can you send me the details?","optionen":["formal","informal"]},{"nr":3,"text":"Thanks a lot for getting back to me.","optionen":["formal","informal"]},{"nr":4,"text":"I would appreciate further information.","optionen":["formal","informal"]},{"nr":5,"text":"The event was really cool.","optionen":["formal","informal"]},{"nr":6,"text":"Please accept my apologies for the delay.","optionen":["formal","informal"]}],"kategorien":[{"name":"formal","anzahlItems":3},{"name":"informal","anzahlItems":3}],"spaltentitelBegriff":"Expression","spaltentitelKategorie":"Register"},"loesung":{"zuordnung":{"1":["formal"],"2":["informal"],"3":["informal"],"4":["formal"],"5":["informal"],"6":["formal"]}}}

thema: CEFR B2 · Reading strategies for reliable understanding
blockJson: {"id":"b1","punkte":12,"arbeitsanweisung":"Read each question and choose the best answer. Only one answer is correct in each question.","typ":"multipleChoice","config":{"fragen":[{"nr":1,"frage":"What is the most useful first step when approaching a long unfamiliar article?","optionen":[{"key":"A","text":"Read the title, headings and opening carefully to predict the topic."},{"key":"B","text":"Translate every word before reading any sentence."},{"key":"C","text":"Read only the final paragraph."},{"key":"D","text":"Ignore the text structure and focus on punctuation."}],"mehrfach":false},{"nr":2,"frage":"What should a reader do when a single unknown word does not prevent understanding?","optionen":[{"key":"A","text":"Stop immediately and look up every possible meaning."},{"key":"B","text":"Use the surrounding context and continue reading."},{"key":"C","text":"Assume that the word changes the whole topic."},{"key":"D","text":"Skip the entire paragraph."}],"mehrfach":false},{"nr":3,"frage":"Which evidence best supports an interpretation of an author’s attitude?","optionen":[{"key":"A","text":"The reader’s personal opinion about the topic."},{"key":"B","text":"The length of the title alone."},{"key":"C","text":"Repeated word choices, examples and qualifying expressions in the text."},{"key":"D","text":"The number of paragraphs, without considering their content."}],"mehrfach":false},{"nr":4,"frage":"How can a reader check whether a conclusion is justified?","optionen":[{"key":"A","text":"By choosing the most emotional sentence."},{"key":"B","text":"By accepting the first impression without rereading."},{"key":"C","text":"By counting how many adjectives the author uses."},{"key":"D","text":"By comparing the conclusion with the text’s claims and supporting evidence."}],"mehrfach":false}]},"loesung":{"antworten":{"1":["A"],"2":["B"],"3":["C"],"4":["D"]}}}
quelleHinweis: Redaktionell kuratierte Aufgabe für das Fachpaket Englisch Oberstufe (CEFR B1–B2), fiktives Unterrichtsszenario. Kein amtliches Lehrplanmaterial; Lehrplankonformität nicht geprüft.
